# terrible_azure_azcollection_azure_rm_trafficmanagerprofile (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the Traffic Manager profile.
- `resource_group` (String) Name of a resource group where the Traffic Manager profile exists or will be created.

### Optional

- `allowed_endpoint_record_types` (String) The list of allowed endpoint record types.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `dns_config` (String) The DNS settings of the Traffic Manager profile.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `location` (String) Valid Azure location. Defaults to `global` because in default public Azure cloud, Traffic Manager profile can only be deployed globally. Reference https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/traffic-manager/quickstart-create-traffic-manager-profile#create-a-traffic-manager-profile.
- `max_return` (Number) Maximum number of endpoints to be returned for MultiValue routing type.
- `monitor_config` (String) The endpoint monitoring settings of the Traffic Manager profile.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `profile_status` (String) The status of the Traffic Manager profile.
- `routing_method` (String) The traffic routing method of the Traffic Manager profile.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Assert the state of the Traffic Manager profile. Use `present` to create or update a Traffic Manager profile and `absent` to delete it.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `endpoints` (String) List of endpoint IDs attached to the profile.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
